name: Electrolysis of Water and Brine
description: Electrolysis uses an external voltage above the decomposition potential to drive reduction at the cathode and oxidation at the anode; competing anode reactions (O2 vs Cl2) depend on overpotential, and Faraday's laws relate product amounts to charge passed.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Electrolyte + power supply"]
    N2["Apply V > decomposition"]
    N3["Cathode reduction (H2)"]
    N4["Anode oxidation"]
    N5{"Which anode product?"}
    N6["Brine: Cl2 evolved"]
    N7["Water: O2 evolved"]
    N8["Faraday: n ∝ charge"]
    N9["Overpotential adds voltage"]
    N10["Products collected"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N2 --> N4
    N4 --> N5
    N5 -->|brine| N6
    N5 -->|water| N7
    N3 --> N8
    N6 --> N8
    N7 --> N8
    N8 --> N10
    N2 --> N9
    N9 --> N10

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
